channel.title: Deepseek on jamesm.blog
channel.description: Recent content in Deepseek on jamesm.blog

item.title: The Open Weight Models Renaissance: Llama, Mistral, Qwen, DeepSeek
item.description: Open-weight models are no longer the cheap alternative - in 2026 they are credible defaults for many workloads. A walk through where Llama, Mistral, Qwen, and DeepSeek actually sit relative to closed frontier models, and what changed in the last 18 months.

item.title: Reasoning Models in 2026: o3, R2, and the Compute-at-Inference Shift
item.description: A grounded look at the reasoning-model wave - what makes o3, DeepSeek R1/R2, Gemini Deep Think, and Claude Extended Thinking different from the generation that came before, when to actually reach for one, and what the cost and capability trade-offs look like in production.

item.title: DeepSeek 🤯
item.description:
TL;DR

  • DeepSeek’s January 2025 release of R1 shook markets - a frontier-grade reasoning model trained for a reported $6M, a fraction of US lab budgets
  • The app shot to #1 on Apple’s App Store inside days, and the open weights forced an industry-wide rethink of what training really costs
  • Subsequent releases (V3 and beyond) cemented DeepSeek as a serious competitor in the open-source and cost-efficient AI category
  • The story is less “China caught up” and more “the cost floor moved” - implications for closed-model pricing, GPU demand, and open-weight strategy
  • Worth understanding as the moment that made cheap, capable, open models a credible default rather than a curiosity

Overview

Wow, crazy times, the best technology in the world is now becoming incredibly cheap and accessible to everyone! 🤯
